title: TC001-Generate a form from a natural-language prompt (happy path)
description: Verifies a user can generate a form from a prompt on the landing page and is redirected to the editor with generated fields visible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type the prompt into the prompt textarea and click the 'CREATE FORM' button to trigger generation and navigation to the editor. After the page navigates, verify the URL contains '/dashboard/' and '/edit' and verify the generated fields 'Name' and 'Email' are visible, then mark the task done.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div/div/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Create a customer feedback form with: name, email, visit date, rating 1-5, and an open comment field')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Sign in with existing account using email/password redirects to dashboard
description: Verifies that a user can sign in with valid email/password and is redirected to /dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Click the 'LOGIN' button to navigate to the login page (expect URL to contain '/login').
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the LOGIN link (index 626) again to navigate to /login (second attempt).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/login' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Dashboard')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Dashboard' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

ASSERTIONS:

- 'Dashboard' text not visible on the page after signing in.
- Sign In button was clicked but the page did not transition to a dashboard view.
- No visible error message indicating authentication failure was displayed.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Click the 'LOGIN' button on the homepage to open the login/signup page (element index 639).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to /login (http://localhost:3000/login) as the test step requests, then wait for the page to load so the login/signup inputs can be located.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
        
        # -> Type a new unique email address into the email field (index 1290).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ayush17_08+20260315_1530@discord.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('abcd1234')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign Up' button (index 1292) to submit the registration form, then wait for the page to redirect so the dashboard assertions can be checked.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'My Forms' button (index 2138) to navigate to the dashboard so the URL and 'Dashboard' text can be verified.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login (required by test plan).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
        
        # -> Type the email into the email field (index 740). Then type the password into the password field (index 741). Then click the 'Sign In' button (index 742).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ayush17_08@discord.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('abcd1234')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the first form's 'View Analytics' button to open the analytics page (use interactive element index 2270).

        # Click element

        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'analytics-chart')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'analytics-chart' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
